entry.title: Re: Set BUT000-XBLCK = X; lfa1 - sperr = 'X' and lfb1-zah...
entry.content:
I guess the BAdI CVI_CUSTOM_MAPPER may be used ONLY when maintaining a Business Partner to adjust vendor and customer specific data.

When you're maintaining the BP, maybe you could use another BAdI.

See if you can use the method IF_EX_PARTNER_UPDATE~CHANGE_BEFORE_UPDATE of the BAdI PARTNER_UPDATE, read the memory buffer to get BUT000 via the function module BUP_MEMORY_BUT000_GET, set XBLCK = 'X', write the updated data back into the BDT buffer using BUP_MEMORY_BUT000_FILL.

This proposal of changing before the update WAS NOT TESTED.

GenAI was used to help generate this content..

entry.title: Re: Subordinates transfer together with manager
entry.content: @VM90 Why would subordinates move along with the manager, when the manager changes the position? If the subordinates should move together with the manager, you might move the managers' position. But as soon as the position hierarchy is the leading one, the direct reports are still on the positions which are assigned to the managers position. And this doesn't change if you transfer the manager to another position.

entry.title: How can users trace the Address Number displayed in the Purchase Requisition?
entry.content:
How can users trace the Address Number displayed in the Purchase Requisition back to the originating Supplier/Customer Business Partner record?  

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How can users trace the Address Number displayed in t...
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

Thank you for your follow-up.

Regarding the Address Number displayed in the Purchase Requisition, please note that this is an internal address identifier and it is not always directly linked to a Supplier/Customer Business Partner record. Therefore, it cannot be traced directly from the Manage Business Partner Master Data app.

If the delivery address was created using Address Type "User Entry" in the Purchase Requisition, the system creates a new address record with a unique Address Number. These addresses can later appear under Address Type "Reference" and may not exist as an address maintained in the Business Partner master data.

For more details, please refer to the SAP Knowledge Base Article:

KB0991267 – Details regarding 'Address Reference' in PO or Address type 'reference' in PR (SAP Note 0003605938)

As a recommendation, users should select existing addresses using Address Type "Reference" whenever possible to avoid duplicate address entries.

Best regards,
SAP Support Team

entry.title: Error Occured When Deleting a User in the Manage User Assignments App
entry.content:
Dear members,

We are unable to delete a user in the Manage User Assignments app.

Steps to Reproduce:
1. Open the Manage User Assignments app.
2. Manually add a user.
3. Save the user.
4. Select the added user and attempt to delete them.
5. Change the user's status to Inactive and attempt to delete them again.

Actual Result:
The user cannot be deleted, even after their status is changed to Inactive. The following error message is displayed:

"An error occurred during the deletion of the object."

Could you please confirm whether this behavior is expected? If so, please provide the relevant documentation and explain how an incorrectly added or no longer required user should be removed.

Best regards,
Koichi

entry.title: Brains Meet Boundaries: Business Rules and Agentic AI in SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content:
For years, business rules have been the backbone of automation in SAP SuccessFactors. They ensure HR processes remain consistent, compliant, and aligned with organizational policies. Whether it's validating employee data, determining recruiting template or new hire onboarding program, leave eligibility, or routing approvals, business rules have successfully automated countless HR transactions within SuccessFactors. However, the rise of Agentic AI is changing the conversation. Instead of asking, "What happens when a condition is met?", administrators are beginning to ask, "How can an AI agent achieve an HR objective while respecting our policies and governance?". This shift doesn't replace business rules, rather it elevates their role. Today, what we see is Business rules becoming the guardrails that define organizational boundaries while on the other hand, AI agents becoming the intelligent collaborators capable of orchestrating complete business outcomes. Using an objective transition from Rule-Based Automation to Goal-Based Execution, a few use cases are described below:

Business Rule: If

  • {Performance Rating ≥ 4} and {Years of Service ≥ 2}
    Then
  • {Employee is eligible for promotion}

The rule executes exactly as designed. It neither questions nor expands the decision.

AI Agent: Approaches the same scenario differently:

    • Review historical performance trends.
    • Analyze the employee's skills and certifications.
    • Evaluate succession plans.
    • Compare internal pay equity.
    • Check departmental budgets.
    • Prepare the promotion justification.
    • Route approvals.
    • Recommend leadership learning after promotion.

Output: The original business rule still determines eligibility. The AI agent simply manages everything around it to deliver the desired business outcome. This represents one of the most significant shifts in enterprise HR automation.

 

Business Rules Become Governance, Not Workflow - As organizations adopt Agentic AI, business rules evolve from being workflow drivers to becoming governance mechanisms. Instead of controlling every step of a process, they define what an AI agent is allowed to do.

Use Case 1: Hiring Process.

Traditional SuccessFactors Process

  • Validate mandatory employee information.
  • Check duplicate employee records.
  • Assign onboarding tasks.
  • Route approvals.

Agentic AI-Enabled Process
The hiring manager simply says: "Hire Priya as a Senior Consultant starting August 1."
The AI agent:

    • Collects missing information from the hiring manager.
    • Validates mandatory business rules.
    • Recommends the appropriate job classification.
    • Creates onboarding activities.
    • Coordinates IT equipment requests.
    • Books induction sessions.
    • Notifies payroll and facilities.
    • Monitors completion of onboarding tasks.

Output: You will observe that throughout the process, the AI agent never bypasses business rules. Instead, it orchestrates multiple systems while remaining compliant with organizational policies.

 

Use Case 2: Leave Management

Traditional Business Rule: {If leave balance is sufficient, approve the request.}

Agentic AI: {The AI agent evaluates}:

  • Team availability.
  • Project deadlines.
  • Public holidays.
  • Historical leave patterns.
  • Manager preferences.

Output: If the requested dates conflict with a critical project, the AI agent might recommend alternative dates that satisfy both employee preferences and business needs, while explaining the reasoning to the employee and manager.

 

Use Case 3: Internal Mobility

While business rules validate employee eligibility before applying for an internal role in recruiting, Agentic AI extends this capability for recruiters: Instead of waiting for employees to search for opportunities, an AI agent can proactively identify individuals whose skills align with upcoming vacancies within the system.

Example: An employee completes multiple cloud certifications.

The AI agent:

  • Detects the new qualifications.
  • Identifies suitable open positions.
  • Recommends additional learning to close remaining skill gaps.
  • Notifies the employee about relevant opportunities.
  • Informs HR of potential internal candidates.

Output: The business rules still determine eligibility, but the AI agent creates a far more proactive talent experience

 

Use Case 4: Cross-Module HR Orchestration

One limitation of traditional business rules is that they often operate within a single application or module, using an employee relocation to illustrate this difference.

Traditional Automation: When an employee transfers from London to Singapore:

  • Update legal entity.
  • Change payroll information.
  • Modify benefits.
  • Update organizational assignment.

Agentic AI: The AI agent can also:

  • Creates a relocation checklist.
  • Schedules tax compliance activities.
  • Recommends local cultural training.
  • Notifies facilities.
  • Coordinates IT provisioning.
  • Updates travel profiles.
  • Informs the manager of pending actions.
  • Tracks completion across all systems.

Output: Instead of automating isolated transactions, the AI agent orchestrates the entire business process.

 

Use Case 5: Intelligent Exception Handling

Traditionally, business rules are designed for known scenarios, and they often struggle with exceptions. Consider a rule that rejects candidates under the legal hiring age.

Agentic AI: An AI agent can recognize contextual nuances such as internship programs, country-specific labor regulations, or parental consent requirements. Rather than rejecting the transaction outright, it can request additional documentation or escalate the case to HR for further review.

Output: AI enables recruiters and organisations to handle exceptions more effectively without compromising compliance.

 

Business Values in Effective Governance and System Administration

As AI agents become increasingly autonomous, governance becomes a critical success factor. Organizations should establish clear policies regarding:

  • Which decisions AI agents can make independently.
  • Which decisions require human approval.
  • How AI recommendations are audited.
  • How explanations are presented to users.
  • How role-based security and data privacy are enforced.

The model described above shows that business rules remain the authoritative source of organizational policy, while AI agents operate within those predefined boundaries. The future of SAP SuccessFactors is not about replacing business rules with AI. It is about combining deterministic automation with intelligent orchestration.

Business rules continue to answer: "Is this action allowed?"

Agentic AI answers: "How can I achieve the desired outcome while complying with those rules?"

Organizations that successfully combine these two capabilities will move beyond automating individual HR tasks to orchestrating complete employee journeys from hiring and onboarding to development, mobility, compensation, and retirement. That is where the real value of Agentic AI lies: not in replacing established business processes, but in making them more intelligent, adaptive, and outcome driven.

entry.title: New Partner Content on SAP Business Accelerator Hub (Q2 ’26)
entry.content:
In continuation of our previous blog, we are excited again to share the latest updates and continue delivering valuable insights to our community.

In this edition of our blog series, we highlight the newest content packages (particularly Integrations) released on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub by our partners in Q2 2026, along with high-level details.

For complete details on each partner's content, see the partners listing on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub Home page.

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Incture Technologies Private Limited

Integration package published:

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition Integration with CITIBANK

This package support integration of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition with Citibank through file transfer using PGP Encryption and Decryption. The integration content sends Vendor domestic payment files to the banking system.

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Akeneo

Integration packages published:

SAP ERP integration with Akeneo PIM

Accelerate your integration with SAP ERP by importing product data into Akeneo. This unidirectional package includes prebuilt components and unlocks features for batch synchronization, message mapping, and Akeneo API query. The Akeneo Accelerator for SAP ERP reduces custom development, simplifies integration, and shortens time-to-value.

Akeneo PIM Event Platform integration with SAP ERP

In today’s fast-moving e-commerce landscape, even small delays between product updates in Akeneo and their appearance on your sales channels can mean lost trust, avoidable returns, and missed revenue opportunities. Your business relies on accurate, up-to-date product data reaching every channel without delay.

The Akeneo PIM Event Platform:
This package eliminates that lag. It’s not just a queue. It’s a dedicated, event-driven pipeline that ensures every product change—creation, update, or deletion—is instantly reflected across your connected systems. The result: faster launches, fewer errors, and confident teams working from the same, current source of truth.

The Integrator’s Advantage:
Accelerate delivery with a pre-configured setup that minimizes custom development and complexity. You’ll bring immediate, measurable value to your clients and stand out as the expert who enables real-time data flow with speed and reliability.

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Planon Group B.V.

Integration package published:

Planon Financial Collection Integration for Purchase Requisition with SAP S/4HANA Procurement

This package enables consumption of the scenario - Create OData Purchase Requisitions for multiple destinations in a routed way.

To enable the integration with SAP S/4HANA Procurement, you need to implement the solution process 2XD and require the SAP Real Estate and Facilities Management by Planon solution to be able to send the Purchase Request via the Financial Collection process.

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Veritas Prime LLC

Integration package published:

Third Party Scheduling Application to SAP SuccessFactors Integration

Integration package providing best‑practice patterns for replicating shift schedules from third‑party scheduling applications to SAP SuccessFactors Time Management, including reusable Work Schedule Day Models and accurate employee planned working time via Temporary Time Information.

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Thank you very much for your attention, and we look forward to sharing more exciting content in the near future.

If you are interested in Partnering with SAP Business Accelerator Hub, please refer to this section for more details.

entry.title: What's New in Quality Management — SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I am excited to share the latest innovations for Quality Management from the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608 release. This update delivers targeted enhancements designed to improve compliance, operational efficiency, and traceability across your quality management processes.

 

Manage Usage Decisions

  • Change documents for relevant quality management UIs
  • Input option for batches in the Manage Usage Decisions app
  • Integration of batch status in the Manage Usage Decisions app

Nonconformance Management

  • Complaint against supplier: creating complaint from the manage defects worklist

Serial Number Tracking

  • Defect enablement for serial numbers

What's New in the Manage Usage Decisions App

Change Documents for Relevant Quality Management UIs

For industries operating under strict regulatory frameworks — pharmaceuticals, food processing, medical devices, chemical manufacturing — every change to a quality-relevant record must be documented, traceable, and auditable. With release 2608, change documentation is now embedded directly into the relevant SAP Fiori Quality Management apps.

The Manage Usage Decisions app and the Record Results for Inspection Points app both receive a dedicated Changes Section, showing exactly what was changed, when, and by whom — without leaving the application. An auditor reviewing a batch release decision can immediately see the full history of that usage decision record, directly within the app.

 

For more information, see: Manage Usage Decisions: Changes Section

Input Option for Batches in the Manage Usage Decisions App

In make-to-stock production and other scenarios involving late-stage batch creation, it is common for an inspection lot to exist before its corresponding batch is created. Previously, users could see the batch field but could not assign or update it from within the Manage Usage Decisions app.

With 2608, batch field editing is now enabled for exactly these scenarios — allowing quality personnel to assign the batch directly without switching to another transaction. The batch field is only editable where no batch has yet been assigned, protecting data integrity.

 

For more information, see: Manage Usage Decisions: View Batch Data and Assign Batches

Integration of Batch Status in the Manage Usage Decisions App

This enhancement prominently surfaces three critical batch fields within the Manage Usage Decisions app: Batch, Batch Status (unrestricted, restricted, or blocked), and Best Before End Date.

When a quality inspector makes a usage decision, this context is now available on the same screen — without navigating to separate transactions. This is particularly valuable for food and beverage, pharmaceuticals, and life sciences, where shelf life is a critical quality parameter.

 

For more information, see: Manage Usage Decisions: View Batch Data and Assign Batches

What's New in Nonconformance Management

Complaint Against Supplier: Creating a Complaint from the Manage Defects Worklist

When a defect involving a supplier-delivered material is identified, the next step is often a formal complaint against that supplier. Previously, this required exiting the Manage Defects app, navigating to a separate transaction, and manually re-entering defect information.

With 2608, you can now create a complaint against a supplier directly from the Manage Defects worklist — from a single defect or a multi-selection. The complaint is pre-populated with the relevant defect context, and multiple defects from the same delivery can be consolidated into one complaint.

 

For more information, see: Processing Complaints Against Suppliers

What's New in Serial Number Tracking

Defect Enablement for Serial Numbers

For industries tracking individual product units — industrial machinery, automotive, high-tech, medical devices — the connection between a serial number and its quality history is critical for service decisions, warranty management, and regulatory compliance.

With 2608, the Serial Number History app now displays defect records linked to specific serial numbers. Quality engineers can see a complete defect history for an individual unit and navigate with a single click into the Process Defects app — providing end-to-end traceability: Serial Number → Defect History → Defect Processing.

 

For more information, see: View Defect Details in the Serial Number History App

Stay Connected

Don't hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts.

More innovation can be found in the What's New Viewer.

Watch the replay of our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608 Early Release Series!

From July 13–17, we hosted expert-led live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the 2608 release. Watch the Quality Management session on demand — anywhere, anytime.

entry.title: What's New in Advanced Variant Configuration — SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I am excited to share the latest innovations for Advanced Variant Configuration (AVC) from the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608 release. This update delivers three enhancements covering performance, API extensibility, and a new modeling environment for product bundles.

 

Advanced Variant Configuration

  • Performance improvements for variant tables
  • Additional AVC capabilities in Sales Order OData V4 APIs
  • Bundle modeling environment: Manage Solution Bundles

What’s New in Advanced Variant Configuration

Performance Improvements for Variant Tables

Products configured using Advanced Variant Configuration often rely on large variant tables to define valid combinations of characteristic values. As these tables grow in size and complexity, load times during configuration sessions can become a bottleneck — slowing down sales order entry, quotation creation, and configuration validation.

With the 2608 release, SAP delivers targeted performance improvements for variant tables in AVC. Users working with large variant tables will experience faster load times and improved responsiveness during configuration sessions. This is particularly impactful for manufacturers with highly configurable product portfolios where variant tables contain thousands of rows.

For more information, see: Performance Improvements for Variant Tables

Additional AVC Capabilities in Sales Order OData V4 APIs

Integration between AVC and external systems — such as third-party CPQ solutions, e-commerce platforms, and ERP-to-ERP interfaces — relies on programmatic access to sales order configuration data. Gaps in API coverage force workarounds or limit what integration partners can do within the standard.

With 2608, additional Advanced Variant Configuration fields and operations are now exposed via the Sales Order OData V4 APIs. This enables richer integration scenarios for sales order creation and processing, including enhanced access to AVC configuration data during order simulation and processing workflows. The result is a more complete and extensible API surface for teams building touchless order entry or hybrid landscape integrations.

For more information, see: Advanced Variant Configuration for Sales Documents

Bundle Modeling Environment: Manage Solution Bundles

Variant configuration modeling has traditionally required deep product expertise — knowledge of characteristics, classes, configuration profiles, and dependency logic. This creates a bottleneck: only a small group of specialized modelers can create or update the configuration objects that represent your product portfolio.

With the 2608 release, SAP introduces the Manage Solution Bundles app — a dedicated bundle modeling environment within Advanced Variant Configuration. The app is designed specifically for business users who are not familiar with the terminology and methodology of Variant Configuration modeling. It abstracts the underlying complexity and presents a guided, intuitive interface for working with solution bundles.

Key capabilities of the new app include:

  • Search and display existing solution bundles
  • Create new solution bundles
  • Edit existing solution bundles
  • Delete existing solution bundles
  • Publish solution bundles

This brings bundle management closer to the product and sales teams who understand the business logic — without requiring them to work in the underlying configuration layer.

 

For more information, see: Manage Solution Bundles

Stay Connected

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts.

More innovation can be found in the What’s New Viewer.

Watch the replay of our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608 Early Release Series!

From July 13–17, we hosted expert-led live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the 2608 release. Watch the Advanced Variant Configuration session on demand — anywhere, anytime.

entry.title: Direct Tendering for Ocean Freight Bookings in SAP TM S/4HANA 2025
entry.content:
SAP indicates in its official product documentation and says "You can use only the direct tendering process for ocean freight bookings." (source: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/e3dc5400c1cc41d1bc0ae0e7fd9aa5a2/c336c907db3742eeb97e9138dd419970.html#use ), but:

  • Tendering settings section are not available on Define Freight Booking Document Type customization as it is available for Freight Orders.
  • Tendering Overview section is not available on Freight Booking document UI.
  • Automatic Tendering button is not available on Freight Booking document UI.
  • Even if you manage to start tendering (via TC buttons) for Ocean Bookings system sends TransportationOrderRequest_Out as B2B message instead of expected TransportationOrderBookingRequest_Out. Also in normal subcontracting process, "Send to Carrier" button triggers TransportationOrderBookingRequest_Out for Freight Bookings. This irony same with cancelation messages too.

All these are expected or SAP is missing with some corrections?

All the tests and checks are done in SAP S/4HANA 2025 FPS1 product version.

Best Regards,

Kaya

entry.title: Re: Important Notice: Changes in SAP Analytics Cloud Classic Stories & Analytic Applications

entry.title: If only the incumbent of a related Matrix Position changes, no sync triggered to job relations
entry.content:
Currently, Job Relationship synchronization is triggered only when the employee's Position assignment, the Position Matrix Relationship, or the Higher Level Position changes.

If only the incumbent of a related Matrix Position or Higher Level Position changes, no synchronization is triggered, and Job Relationships continue to reference the previous incumbent.

This results in outdated manager/reporting relationships, workflows going to incorrect approvers, inconsistent organizational data, and additional manual effort to keep Job Relationships up to date.

SAP Support confirmed that this is the current standard behavior and recommended submitting an enhancement request if automatic synchronization on incumbent changes is required. (which we did, please vote if you like the idea: Auto sync Job Relationships when the incumbent of a related Matrix or Higher Level Position changed.)

I was quite surprised when we realized that there is no standard feature for that as I assumed that this is a standard requirement from the customers using EC. 

I would like to ask you to share your current solutions to keep the job relations up to date in the absence of this functionality.

Thank you,

Linda

entry.title: EWM Physical Inventory: Error "User is not assigned to any tolerance group for clearing"
entry.content:
Hi EWM Experts,

I am testing an Ad-hoc Physical Inventory (Storage-Bin-Specific) scenario in SAP EWM integrated with S/4HANA.

During testing, when I enter a physical count that results in a variance (either a surplus or loss) and attempt to save/post in transaction /SCWM/PI_PROCESS, the system blocks the action with the following error log:

  • Error: User <USER_ID> is not assigned to any tolerance group for clearing

  • Warning: Document <PI_DOC_NUM>, item 000001 cannot be posted due to exceeded tolerances

Observations:

  1. If the counted quantity matches the book quantity perfectly (Zero Difference), the document posts without issues.

  2. Any variance (even 1EA gain or loss) triggers this tolerance error.

Questions:

  1. What is the standard SPRO path to configure physical inventory tolerance groups for difference clearing?

  2. How are tolerance limits evaluated between /SCWM/PI_PROCESS and Difference Analyzer /SCWM/DIFF_ANALYZER?

Thanks in advance for your guidance!

  • SWF_WORKFLOW_CONDITION_EVAL

Parameter contains only:

  • shorttext

  • mandatory

Result

Configured in SWF_WORKFLOW_CONDITION_DEF:

service_path
entity
property

Result

Instead of the custom CDS, I used the standard workflow service:

  • EntitySet: C_PurReqnTypeForWorkflow
  • Property: PurchasingDocumentType

I verified from the $metadata that:

  • Service exists
  • EntitySet exists

However, after assigning the property in the parameter definition, the workflow UI no longer displays the input field.

Summary of my observations

Configuration Result
id + name + mandatory Input field displayed
+ ddic_typeInput field displayed
+ service_pathInput field displayed
+ entity Input field displayed
+ propertyInput field disappears completely

It appears that the Workflow framework rejects the parameter once the property is specified.

My Question

Has anyone successfully implemented an F4 Value Help for a custom workflow condition parameter in SWF_WORKFLOW_CONDITION_DEF?

If yes, could you please share:

  1. Whether a standard or custom OData service was used?
  2. Does the property require additional ValueList/Common.ValueList annotations?
  3. Is F4 Help currently supported for custom workflow condition parameters in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, or is this a framework limitation?

Any guidance would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

entry.title: Understanding the Difference: Book Stock Snapshot vs. Count Entry in SAP EWM Physical Inventory
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

In standard SAP EWM integrated with S/4HANA, the Physical Inventory (PI) lifecycle follows a structured path between EWM warehouse activities and S/4HANA IM financial postings. Here is a brief overview of how stock variances move through the system:

1. Document Activation & Stock Freeze

When a PI Document is created and Activated (/SCWM/PI_PROCESS), EWM locks the storage bin to prevent stock movements and takes a Book Inventory Snapshot.

2. Count Entry

Counts can be captured via GUI (/SCWM/PI_PROCESS) or RF Scanner (/SCWM/RFUI).

  • The system calculates the variance using:

    $$\text{Counted Quantity} - \text{Book Inventory} = \text{Difference Quantity}$$

  • If Blind Counting is active in customization, expected stock quantities remain hidden from counters to ensure data integrity.

3. Difference Posting & Clearing

  • EWM Posting (/SCWM/PI_PROCESS😞 Releases the storage bin lock and updates EWM physical stock. Requires user mapping to a Physical Inventory Tolerance Group.

  • S/4HANA IM Clearing (/SCWM/DIFF_ANALYZER😞 Posts inventory adjustment movement types (e.g., 711 for gains, 712 for losses) to synchronize financial stock valuation.

What processes or authorization controls do you use in your warehouse implementations? Looking forward to hearing your insights!

entry.title: Re: Sales Order exceeds Credit Limit is NOT automatically...
entry.content:
Dear @thongle3103,

Yes. Based on the described test scenario this is standard Public Cloud behavior

With update group 000012 the credit exposure is updated starting at the sales-order stage. The sales order increases the open order value from delivery-relevant schedule lines. Therefore, when Sales Order A with a value of 1200 is created against a credit limit of 1000, the order can be blocked immediately

With update group 000015 the credit exposure update starts at the delivery stage. The sales order itself does not increase the open order credit exposure. The delivery subsequently increases the open delivery value, followed by the billing document value and financial open items

Therefore, assuming this is the first transaction for the customer and there is no other existing credit exposure, Sales Order B is not expected to be blocked solely because its value exceeds the credit limit. When the outbound delivery is created, its value becomes part of the credit exposure and the delivery can be blocked by the configured delivery-level credit check

Consequently the observed result is expected:

  • Segment 2000 with update group 000012: credit block at sales-order creation
  • Segment 1000 with update group 000015: no block at sales-order creation but credit block at delivery creation

To include delivery-related sales orders in credit exposure and enforce the credit limit already during sales-order creation update group 000012 should be used

More details below:

SAP KBA 3587422 – The Credit Update Groups in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: How do I set up the “Test Automation Tool” in SAP S/4HANA Cloud?
entry.content:
Hello @soerenlangedeloitte ,

There are couple of approaches to handle the case of adding test user in TAT based on IAS and system configuration.

  1. If the login happens through custom IDP then (for example Azure or OKTA) then in IAS there is a section called conditional URL where special URL is maintained. It's required to allow the login using conditional URL option and maintain this URL in test your process app. More details for this process is maintained in the link - https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2ab07d21f68c41109a2eef21b8fd8466/5d867592753c465aadb81115de672f91.html?version=2608.500&ai=true 
  2. If the login screen has option to enter username in SAP provided identity provider and later based on rules if it's taking to other IDPs then it's required to create rule in conditional URL to allow login to happen using SAP IDP for specific user/user group. More details on this process can be found in the link - https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2ab07d21f68c41109a2eef21b8fd8466/5d867592753c465aadb81115de672f91.html?state=DRAFT&ai=true 

If you need more details on this part, do get back to our team

Best Regards,

Naveen

entry.title: Re: EWM Physical Inventory: Error "User is not assigned t...
entry.content:
Hello @sureshchalapathy 

Tolerance groups are defined in SPRO: SCM Extended Warehouse Management -> Extended Warehouse Management -> Internal Warehouse Processes -> Physical Inventory -> Warehouse-Number-Specific Settings -> Define Tolerance Groups -> ...

Then they area assigned to user ids in SAP menu: Logistics -> SCM Extended Warehouse Management  -> Extended Warehouse Management  -> Settings -> Physical Inventory -> ...

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Automating SAP Security Tasks and SoD Analysis with ZSecTools (Open-Source)
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

As SAP Security administrators, we all know that keeping an ABAP stack secure, compliant, and clean involves a lot of repetitive tasks, custom queries, and sometimes a bit of frustration. While enterprise governance solutions manage high-level corporate compliance, security professionals on the ground often need a localized, agile set of utilities for immediate daily operations and deep-dive analysis.

That is why I created ZSecTools—an open-source toolkit designed specifically to support SAP Security professionals in their day-to-day activities.

The tool was originally developed for personal use to support day-to-day SAP security administration and SOD analysis work. It is now shared as open source in the hope that it can be useful to others facing similar needs.

I've been working in the SAP security field since 2011, and I've come to realize that I would like to share my knowledge with the community to create and share value.

You can find the full source code and documentation on GitHub: 👉 https://github.com/va87git/zsectools

 

What is ZSecTools?

ZSecTools is not a certified governance tool, nor is it meant to replace robust enterprise risk management software. Instead, think of it as a professional Swiss Army knife. It is built to provide immediate added value, automating heavy tasks and offering deep visibility into the security authorization model of your SAP systems.

 

Architecture Overview & Deployment

To keep deployment simple, clean, and isolated, ZSecTools leverages a modern architecture that can be run in two different ways depending on your environment:

  • Backend & Processing: Powered by a Node.js engine that handles the heavy lifting, including complex SoD calculations and mass operations.

  • Data Layer: Uses a localized PostgreSQL database to store configuration matrices, imported data, and report results securely.

  • Flexible Deployment Options:

    • Dockerized Stack: You can spin up the full environment with a simple container setup to keep everything isolated.

    • Self-Contained / Portable Scripts: If you cannot or prefer not to use Docker, the repository includes automation scripts that use curl to pull down portable versions of Node.js and PostgreSQL. This creates a fully local, self-contained installation that doesn't require administrator privileges or permanent system installations.

  • SAP Connectivity (BYOD): To connect to your ABAP backend, the tool relies on a Bring Your Own Dependencies approach. Out of respect for SAP licensing compliance, the repository does not include proprietary files. Users must download the required SAP NW RFC SDK libraries directly from the SAP Me portal using their own S-User account, as detailed in the README setup instructions.

 

Key Features & Capabilities

  • Segregation of Duties (SoD) Analysis: Run targeted SoD checks directly through the toolkit to identify conflicting access risks within your user base.

  • Custom Security Quality Reports: The tool includes several built-in custom reports designed to measure and audit the overall quality and health of your SAP security configuration.

  • Mass Maintenance Operations: Managing roles and users one by one can be time-consuming. ZSecTools includes powerful mass functionalities to streamline user and role administration.

  • License Exposure Estimation (STAR-aligned): By uploading a properly configured SoD matrix, you can run a report to estimate your license risk profile based on critical permissions. Since the official STAR matrix is publicly available on the SAP Support Portal, you can easily map these criteria within the tool to evaluate your system's license exposure ahead of official measurements.

 

Pre-configured SAP Role Included

To make the deployment straightforward, the repository includes a dedicated SAP role export file. Importing this file provides a pre-configured role with the minimum authorization objects and RFC call permissions required for the toolkit to function safely, allowing you to review exactly what the tool can access before running it.

 

Designed for Practitioners

I want to emphasize that ZSecTools is built to support people who already have a solid understanding of SAP authorization objects and security concepts. It acts as an accelerator for your existing skills, giving you the technical utility you need to manage your systems more efficiently.

 

Get Involved & Active Development

The project is published under an open-source license and is currently under active development. This means new features, optimizations, and reports are planned for the near future.

Feel free to explore the repository, try it out in your sandbox environments, and share your thoughts or open an issue on GitHub if you have suggestions or find it useful!

entry.title: Integrating GitHub with SAP Integration Suite: A Complete Guide to Version Control and DevOps
entry.content:
Connect GitHub with SAP Integration Suite (Cloud Integration)

Introduction


During my recent work with SAP Integration Suite, I looked into the built-in GitHub integration feature in Cloud Integration. While handling integration projects, I noticed that keeping track of version history, collaborating with several developers, and retrieving previous versions of integration artifacts became difficult when those artifacts were only managed within the tenant. This made me want to explore GitHub integration and see how it connects SAP Integration Suite development with current DevOps practices.

This blog shares my firsthand experience setting up GitHub with SAP Integration Suite. I detail each configuration step and offer practical insights and useful tips I discovered during the implementation.

Why GitHub Integration?
GitHub integration provides version control, collaboration, traceability, backup, governance, and CI/CD readiness for SAP Integration Suite artifacts. Teams can manage iFlows, script collections, and other related artifacts using familiar Git workflows.

By integrating GitHub with SAP Integration Suite, organizations can:

  • Maintain version history of integration artifacts.

  • Enable collaboration among multiple developers.

  • Support branching and release management strategies.

  • Implement CI/CD pipelines.

  • Maintain backup and recovery mechanisms.

  • Improve governance and auditability of changes.

In this blog, we will walk through the complete process of:

  • Creating a GitHub Personal Access Token.

  • Creating and configuring a GitHub repository.

  • Establishing connectivity between SAP Integration Suite and GitHub.

  • Performing Git operations such as Push, Pull, and Import.

  • Understanding best practices for managing integration artifacts using Git.

Why Use GitHub with SAP Integration Suite?

Traditionally, integration artifacts were managed directly within SAP Integration Suite, making collaboration and version management challenging in larger projects.

Git integration solves these challenges by introducing capabilities such as:

Feature Benefit

Version ControlTrack every modification to integration artifacts
CollaborationMultiple developers can work simultaneously
Backup & RecoveryRepository acts as a central backup
GovernanceBetter control using branching strategies
AuditabilityComplete history of changes
CI/CD IntegrationEnables automated deployment pipelines

Git integration becomes extremely useful in enterprise projects where multiple developers work across various environments such as DEV, QA, UAT, and PROD.

Architecture Overview

SAP Integration Suite (Cloud Integration)
                |
                | HTTPS + Personal Access Token
                |
           GitHub Repository
                |
      ----------------------
      |         |          |
    main      develop    feature/*

Integration artifacts such as Integration Flows, Script Collections, Value Mappings, and Message Mappings are stored inside Git repositories and can be synchronized between SAP Integration Suite and GitHub.

Prerequisites

Before proceeding, ensure the following prerequisites are met.

SAP Prerequisites

  • Access to SAP Integration Suite – Cloud Integration.

  • User must have the PI_Administrator role assigned.

  • Appropriate authorizations to create and modify integration artifacts.

GitHub Prerequisites

  • A valid GitHub account.

  • Internet connectivity to access GitHub.

  • Permission to create repositories and generate Personal Access Tokens.

Step 1 – Create a Personal Access Token in GitHub

SAP Integration Suite communicates with GitHub using Git APIs. Since GitHub no longer supports password-based authentication for Git operations, authentication must be performed using a Personal Access Token (PAT).

The Personal Access Token acts as a secure credential that authorizes SAP Integration Suite to perform operations such as:

  • Read repository information

  • Push integration artifacts

  • Pull latest changes

  • Import artifacts from repositories

Navigate to GitHub Settings

Login to GitHub and navigate to:

Profile → Settings

 

Open Developer Settings

Navigate to:

Settings → Developer Settings

 

Create a Personal Access Token

Navigate to:

Personal Access Tokens → Tokens (Classic)

Click:

Generate New Token (Classic)

 

Configure the Token

Provide:

  • Token Name

  • Expiration Date

  • Required Permissions (Scopes)

For testing purposes, all repository-related scopes can be selected. However, in productive environments, it is recommended to grant only the minimum permissions required by following the Principle of Least Privilege (PoLP).

Typical scopes include:

  • repo

  • workflow

  • read:org

Important Note

The generated token is displayed only once by GitHub.

Ensure that you copy and securely store the token because it cannot be viewed again once the page is closed.

This token will be used while configuring Git access in SAP Integration Suite.

 

 

Step 2 – Create a Repository

A Git repository acts as the central storage location for all integration artifacts.

Every time an artifact is pushed from SAP Integration Suite, GitHub stores:

  • Source code

  • Version history

  • Commit information

  • Metadata

  • Change logs

This repository can then be used for:

  • Collaboration

  • Backup

  • Recovery

  • Audit purposes

  • CI/CD integration

Navigate to the GitHub home page and click:

New Repository

Provide:

  • Repository Name

  • Description

  • Visibility (Public or Private)

For enterprise projects, it is recommended to use Private repositories since integration artifacts may contain business logic and sensitive information.

Example:

SAP-CPI-Integration-Artifacts

Step 3 – Create a Branch

Git uses branches to isolate development activities.

Since SAP Integration Suite performs operations against a particular branch, at least one branch must exist.

The easiest way to create the default branch is:

  1. Create a README file.

  2. Commit the file.

GitHub automatically creates the main branch.

 

 

Why Branching is Important

A proper branching strategy enables:

  • Parallel development

  • Controlled releases

  • Easier rollback

  • Better governance

  • Reduced conflicts

A typical enterprise branching strategy looks like:

Branch Purpose

mainProduction-ready artifacts
developIntegration testing
feature/*Development activities
releasePre-production validation
hotfixProduction bug fixes

GitHub Setup Completed

At this stage, we have successfully completed all the necessary configurations on GitHub.

The next step is to establish connectivity between GitHub and SAP Integration Suite.

Step 4 – Configure Git Access in SAP Integration Suite

Navigate to:

Settings → Git Repositories

SAP Integration Suite provides a centralized location for managing Git repository connections.

As of now, SAP Integration Suite allows configuring up to 50 Git repository connections.

This capability is useful when organizations maintain:

  • Project-specific repositories

  • Shared repositories

  • Customer-specific repositories

  • Environment-specific repositories

Click:

Edit → Add

Provide:

Field Value

Repository URLGitHub Repository URL
Access TokenGitHub Personal Access Token

Click:

Add → Save

Once saved, SAP Integration Suite validates the credentials and establishes a secure connection with GitHub.

Git Operations in SAP Integration Suite

After the repository connection is established, SAP Integration Suite supports three major Git operations:

  1. Git Push

  2. Git Pull

  3. Git Import

Git Push

The Git Push operation uploads integration artifacts from SAP Integration Suite into GitHub.

This operation is generally performed when:

  • A new artifact is created.

  • Existing artifacts are modified.

  • Development work is completed.

  • Changes need to be backed up.

Navigate to:

Actions → Git Push

 

Provide:

  • Access Token

  • Repository

  • Branch

  • Commit Message

 

Example commit messages:

Initial version of Customer Integration Interface
Added retry mechanism for SFTP processing
Enhanced mapping for Sales Order Interface

Benefits of Git Push

  • Creates version history.

  • Enables collaboration.

  • Maintains backup.

  • Supports CI/CD pipelines.

  • Improves governance.

After a successful push, Git information becomes visible in the integration artifact.

Git Pull

Git Pull is used to synchronize the latest version of the artifact from GitHub into SAP Integration Suite.

This is useful when:

  • Another developer has modified the artifact.

  • Changes have been merged into the repository.

  • Local artifacts need to be updated.

Navigate to:

Actions → Git Pull

Provide:

  • Access Token

  • Repository

  • Branch

 

 

Important Consideration

Pull operations can fail due to version conflicts.

For example:

  • Local artifact is already modified.

  • Repository version is different.

  • Local version is ahead of repository version.

Therefore, it is recommended to perform Pull operations regularly before starting development.

Git Import

Git Import allows developers to import artifacts directly from Git repositories into another package or tenant.

This feature is extremely useful in scenarios such as:

Tenant Migration

Moving integration artifacts between tenants.

Reusability

Importing common frameworks and templates.

Project Onboarding

New team members can quickly access existing interfaces.

Disaster Recovery

Rebuilding an Integration Suite tenant from repository content.

Navigate to:

Actions → Git Import

Provide:

  • Access Token

  • Repository

  • Branch

SAP Integration Suite displays all available artifacts in the selected branch.

Choose:

  • Add

  • Add and Open in Editor

depending on your requirement.

 

Recommended Git Strategy for SAP Integration Suite

A recommended branching model is:

main      → Production
release   → UAT
develop   → SIT
feature/* → Development

This approach provides:

  • Better governance

  • Parallel development

  • Easier rollback

  • Controlled deployments

  • Reduced production risks

My Experience

While testing the feature, I initially created an empty repository and found that Git operations failed because no branch existed. Creating and committing a README file automatically generated the main branch and resolved the issue. I also found that meaningful commit messages and feature branches significantly improve collaboration when multiple developers work on the same integration package.

Practical Tips from My Testing

Based on my testing, here are a few recommendations:

  • Verify that the Personal Access Token has the required repository permissions before configuring the connection.
  • Ensure the repository already contains a valid branch before attempting Git operations.
  • Use meaningful commit messages to simplify future troubleshooting and change tracking.
  • Perform a Git Pull before starting development if multiple developers are working on the same artifact.
  • Use feature branches instead of developing directly on the main branch for better version control and collaboration.

 

Conclusion

Exploring GitHub integration with SAP Integration Suite gave me a better understanding of how version control can be seamlessly incorporated into the integration development lifecycle. The feature not only simplifies collaboration among developers but also introduces better governance, traceability, and backup capabilities for integration artifacts.

Although the initial setup requires a few configuration steps, once completed, Git operations such as Push, Pull, and Import become straightforward and significantly improve day-to-day development activities. I hope the steps and practical observations shared in this blog help others configure GitHub integration more efficiently and encourage them to adopt version control as part of their SAP Integration Suite development process.

Special thanks to @ShraddhaDas151  for the continuous support, guidance, and valuable insights that contributed to the preparation of this blog.

Thank you for reading this blog. Feel free to share your feedback, suggestions, or questions in the comments section below. Your
inputs are highly appreciated and contribute to continuous l

entry.title: Uploading Multi-Sheet Excel Files to Create Bank Master Records in SAP
entry.content:
INTRODUCTION

The opening  A while back change to your real project type and rough timing (e.g., "During a recent S/4HANA migration project for a client..." or "As part of a support ticket raised by the finance team...").

The bolded sentence about ALSM_EXCEL_TO_INTERNAL_TABLE — delete it if you'd never actually used that older method before If that wasn't your real reaction, replace it with whatever actually confused you about Step 2 — maybe it was the worksheet name lookup, or the ASSIGN COMPONENT syntax itself, or something else entirely Only keep this if you genuinely forgot the commit If your real bug was something else — e.g., a wrong bank key format, a BAPI return error you didn't handle, duplicate records, etc. — delete this sentence and write 1-2 lines describing what actually went wrong for you

Solution Review

The report ZPRG_EXCELSHEET2_CRBAPIREC_RS lets a user upload an Excel file containing Sheet1 and Sheet2, reads both into internal tables, matches records across the two sheets, and creates Bank Master records using the standard BAPI BAPI_BANK_CREATE.

Step 1: Selecting and uploading the Excel file.

This part was straightforward — standard file dialog using cl_gui_frontend_services=>file_open_dialog, then uploading the binary content with GUI_UPLOAD, and converting it to XSTRING with SCMS_BINARY_TO_XSTRING since that's the format CL_FDT_XL_SPREADSHEET expects.

CALL METHOD cl_gui_frontend_services=>gui_upload
  EXPORTING
    filename   = lv_file
    filetype   = 'BIN'
  IMPORTING
    filelength = lv_size
  CHANGING
    data_tab   = lt_binary.

CALL FUNCTION 'SCMS_BINARY_TO_XSTRING'
  EXPORTING
    input_length = lv_size
  IMPORTING
    buffer       = lv_xstring
  TABLES
    binary_tab   = lt_binary.

Step 2: Reading both worksheets.

The program uses the SAP standard class CL_FDT_XL_SPREADSHEET to open the Excel file. It first retrieves the worksheet names and then reads Sheet1 and Sheet2 separately. The data from each worksheet is stored in separate internal tables by mapping the Excel columns to the corresponding ABAP structures.

CALL METHOD lo_excel->if_fdt_doc_spreadsheet~get_itab_from_worksheet
  EXPORTING
    worksheet_name = 'Sheet1'
  RECEIVING
    itab           = lo_ref1.

ASSIGN lo_ref1->* TO <lt_generic1>.

LOOP AT <lt_generic1> ASSIGNING <ls_generic1>.
  ASSIGN COMPONENT 1 OF STRUCTURE <ls_generic1> TO <lv_field>.
  IF sy-subrc = 0. ls_sheet1-bank_ctry = <lv_field>. ENDIF.
  ...
ENDLOOP.

I'll be honest — the first time I saw this "generic table + field-symbol + component index" pattern, it felt like more ceremony than I expected for something as simple as "read column 1, 2, 3." But once I understood why — the class has no way of knowing your target structure in advance, since Excel columns aren't typed — it made a lot more sense.

The one thing that tripped me up initially was column order: CL_FDT_XL_SPREADSHEET maps by column position, not header name, so if someone reorders columns in the Excel file (which, let's be honest, business users do all the time), the mapping silently breaks. That's worth calling out clearly to whoever maintains the Excel template.

Sheet2 follows the exact same pattern, just with four columns instead of three (adding city and SWIFT code).

Step 3: Matching records and creating Bank Master entries.

Once both sheets were in internal tables, I looped through Sheet1 and used READ TABLE ... WITH KEY to find the matching row in Sheet2 based on bank country and bank key:

READ TABLE lt_sheet2 INTO ls_sheet2
     WITH KEY bank_ctry = ls_sheet1-bank_ctry
              bank_key  = ls_sheet1-bank_key.

IF sy-subrc = 0.
  gs_address-city       = ls_sheet2-city.
  gs_address-swift_code = ls_sheet2-swift.
ENDIF.

Then calling BAPI_BANK_CREATE, and — this part I added after an early test run — explicitly committing or rolling back based on the return message:

IF ls_return-type CA 'AE'.
  CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_ROLLBACK'.
ELSE.
  CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT'
    EXPORTING
      wait = 'X'.
ENDIF.

I actually forgot the commit in my very first version and spent a few confused minutes wondering why none of the banks were showing up in FI01/FI03 afterward — small reminder that BAPIs in SAP generally don't auto-commit, and it's easy to overlook when you're focused on getting the Excel-reading logic right.

Complete ABAP Source Code

 

REPORT zprg_excelsheet2_crbapirec_rs.

TABLES: bnka.

TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_sheet1,
         bank_ctry TYPE bnka-banks,
         bank_key  TYPE bnka-bankl,
         bank_name TYPE bnka-banka,
       END OF ty_sheet1.

TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_sheet2,
         bank_ctry TYPE bnka-banks,
         bank_key  TYPE bnka-bankl,
         city      TYPE bnka-ort01,
         swift     TYPE bnka-swift,
       END OF ty_sheet2.

DATA:
  lt_sheet1  TYPE TABLE OF ty_sheet1,
  ls_sheet1  TYPE ty_sheet1,

  lt_sheet2  TYPE TABLE OF ty_sheet2,
  ls_sheet2  TYPE ty_sheet2,

  lt_binary  TYPE solix_tab,
  lv_xstring TYPE xstring,
  lv_size    TYPE i,

  lo_excel   TYPE REF TO cl_fdt_xl_spreadsheet,
  lt_names   TYPE if_fdt_doc_spreadsheet=>t_worksheet_names,

  ls_return  TYPE bapiret2,
  gs_address TYPE bapi1011_address,

  lv_file    TYPE string,

  lt_files   TYPE filetable,
  lv_rc      TYPE i.

PARAMETERS: p_file TYPE rlgrap-filename.

AT SELECTION-SCREEN ON VALUE-REQUEST FOR p_file.
  PERFORM f4_file_selection.

START-OF-SELECTION.
  PERFORM upload_file.
  PERFORM parse_excel.
  PERFORM create_banks.

FORM f4_file_selection.

  CALL METHOD cl_gui_frontend_services=>file_open_dialog
    EXPORTING
      window_title      = 'Select Excel File'
      default_extension = 'XLSX'
      file_filter       = 'Excel Files (*.xlsx)|*.xlsx|All Files (*.*)|*.*'
    CHANGING
      file_table        = lt_files
      rc                = lv_rc
    EXCEPTIONS
      OTHERS            = 1.

  IF sy-subrc = 0 AND lines( lt_files ) > 0.
    READ TABLE lt_files INTO DATA(ls_file) INDEX 1.
    p_file = ls_file-filename.
  ENDIF.

ENDFORM.

FORM upload_file.

  lv_file = p_file.

  CALL METHOD cl_gui_frontend_services=>gui_upload
    EXPORTING
      filename   = lv_file
      filetype   = 'BIN'
    IMPORTING
      filelength = lv_size
    CHANGING
      data_tab   = lt_binary
    EXCEPTIONS
      OTHERS     = 1.

  IF sy-subrc <> 0.
    MESSAGE 'File upload failed' TYPE 'E'.
  ENDIF.

  CALL FUNCTION 'SCMS_BINARY_TO_XSTRING'
    EXPORTING
      input_length = lv_size
    IMPORTING
      buffer       = lv_xstring
    TABLES
      binary_tab   = lt_binary.

ENDFORM.

FORM parse_excel.

  DATA: lo_ref1 TYPE REF TO data,
        lo_ref2 TYPE REF TO data.

  FIELD-SYMBOLS: <lt_generic1> TYPE STANDARD TABLE,
                 <ls_generic1> TYPE any,
                 <lt_generic2> TYPE STANDARD TABLE,
                 <ls_generic2> TYPE any,
                 <lv_field>    TYPE any.

  TRY.
      CREATE OBJECT lo_excel
        EXPORTING
          document_name = lv_file
          xdocument     = lv_xstring.

      CALL METHOD lo_excel->if_fdt_doc_spreadsheet~get_worksheet_names
        IMPORTING
          worksheet_names = lt_names.

      CALL METHOD lo_excel->if_fdt_doc_spreadsheet~get_itab_from_worksheet
        EXPORTING
          worksheet_name = 'Sheet1'
        RECEIVING
          itab           = lo_ref1.

      ASSIGN lo_ref1->* TO <lt_generic1>.

      LOOP AT <lt_generic1> ASSIGNING <ls_generic1>.
        CLEAR ls_sheet1.
        ASSIGN COMPONENT 1 OF STRUCTURE <ls_generic1> TO <lv_field>.
        IF sy-subrc = 0. ls_sheet1-bank_ctry = <lv_field>. ENDIF.
        ASSIGN COMPONENT 2 OF STRUCTURE <ls_generic1> TO <lv_field>.
        IF sy-subrc = 0. ls_sheet1-bank_key = <lv_field>. ENDIF.
        ASSIGN COMPONENT 3 OF STRUCTURE <ls_generic1> TO <lv_field>.
        IF sy-subrc = 0. ls_sheet1-bank_name = <lv_field>. ENDIF.
        APPEND ls_sheet1 TO lt_sheet1.
      ENDLOOP.

      CALL METHOD lo_excel->if_fdt_doc_spreadsheet~get_itab_from_worksheet
        EXPORTING
          worksheet_name = 'Sheet2'
        RECEIVING
          itab           = lo_ref2.

      ASSIGN lo_ref2->* TO <lt_generic2>.

      LOOP AT <lt_generic2> ASSIGNING <ls_generic2>.
        CLEAR ls_sheet2.
        ASSIGN COMPONENT 1 OF STRUCTURE <ls_generic2> TO <lv_field>.
        IF sy-subrc = 0. ls_sheet2-bank_ctry = <lv_field>. ENDIF.
        ASSIGN COMPONENT 2 OF STRUCTURE <ls_generic2> TO <lv_field>.
        IF sy-subrc = 0. ls_sheet2-bank_key = <lv_field>. ENDIF.
        ASSIGN COMPONENT 3 OF STRUCTURE <ls_generic2> TO <lv_field>.
        IF sy-subrc = 0. ls_sheet2-city = <lv_field>. ENDIF.
        ASSIGN COMPONENT 4 OF STRUCTURE <ls_generic2> TO <lv_field>.
        IF sy-subrc = 0. ls_sheet2-swift = <lv_field>. ENDIF.
        APPEND ls_sheet2 TO lt_sheet2.
      ENDLOOP.

    CATCH cx_fdt_excel_core INTO DATA(lx_excel).
      MESSAGE lx_excel->get_text( ) TYPE 'E'.
  ENDTRY.

  IF lt_sheet1 IS INITIAL.
    MESSAGE 'No data found in Sheet1' TYPE 'E'.
  ENDIF.

ENDFORM.

FORM create_banks.

  LOOP AT lt_sheet1 INTO ls_sheet1.

    IF ls_sheet1-bank_ctry IS INITIAL OR ls_sheet1-bank_key IS INITIAL.
      WRITE: / 'Skipped row - missing country/key'.
      CONTINUE.
    ENDIF.

    CLEAR: ls_return, gs_address.

    gs_address-bank_name = ls_sheet1-bank_name.

    READ TABLE lt_sheet2 INTO ls_sheet2
         WITH KEY bank_ctry = ls_sheet1-bank_ctry
                  bank_key  = ls_sheet1-bank_key.

    IF sy-subrc = 0.
      gs_address-city       = ls_sheet2-city.
      gs_address-swift_code = ls_sheet2-swift.
    ENDIF.

    CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_BANK_CREATE'
      EXPORTING
        bank_ctry    = ls_sheet1-bank_ctry
        bank_key     = ls_sheet1-bank_key
        bank_address = gs_address
      IMPORTING
        return       = ls_return.

    IF ls_return-type CA 'AE'.
      CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_ROLLBACK'.
      WRITE: / ls_sheet1-bank_key, ls_return-message.
    ELSE.
      CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT'
        EXPORTING
          wait = 'X'.
      WRITE: / 'Bank Created Successfully:', ls_sheet1-bank_key.
    ENDIF.

  ENDLOOP.

ENDFORM.

CONCLUSION

What I liked about this approach is that it replaces two separate old-style problems — reading Excel data (previously OLE Automation or ALSM_EXCEL_TO_INTERNAL_TABLE) and matching data across sheets — with one clean, modern, generic-table-based method. It's a solid pattern if you ever need to pull structured data from multi-sheet Excel files into any custom or standard BAPI, not just Bank Master creation.

If you've worked with CL_FDT_XL_SPREADSHEET yourself, I'd be curious to hear if you ran into the same column-position mapping issue I did, or if you handled worksheet validation differently. Feel free to share your thoughts, questions, or improvements in the comments below.

SAMPLE INPUT
Sheet1 (Bank Country, Bank Key, Bank Name)

Bank Ctry Bank Key Bank Name

US021000021JPMorgan Chase Bank
US026009593Bank of America
INSBIN0001234State Bank of India
DE10070000Deutsche Bank AG

Sheet2 (Bank Country, Bank Key, City, SWIFT)

Bank Ctry Bank Key City SWIFT

US021000021New YorkCHASUS33
US026009593CharlotteBOFAUS3N
INSBIN0001234MumbaiSBININBB
DE10070000BerlinDEUTDEBB


SAMPLE OUTPUT

Bank Created: 021000021
Bank Created: 026009593
Bank Created: SBIN0001234
Bank Created: 10070000.

ABAP Development

entry.title: Re: Atualizar a Tabela progressiva de IRRF para 2026
entry.content: mesmo com as notas aplicadas e as configurações realizadas o cálculo ainda não funcionou para mim. Abri um chamado na SAP e recebi essa resposta após dizer que o cálculo funcionava antes da nova lei: "Boa tarde Julio, O cálculo funcionava antes pois ele só passava pela tabela progressiva do IRRF. Como a lei nova usa a base bruta, se for alterada a base manualmente o programa não consegue recuperar a base a diferença nesta parte. E se usar INSS na invoice, o programa na hora do pagamento, também não consegue identificar que é para reduzir o valor do INSS. Infelizmente é uma lmitação na solução que temos no momento. Para cenários com desconto simplificado temos um workaround, mas para INSS, que é um valor que é variável, infelizmente a única forma é informar manualmente a base e o imposto. Você pode sugerir para que este cenário seja suportado. Para isto, por favor faça o processo de Customer Influence, descrito na SPA Note 0000000011(https://me.sap.com/notes/0000000011) - Would you like to shape & influence future releases of SAP Products? Com isto, o time responsável irá analisar os requirimentos e possibilidades de realização. Atenciosamente, Bernardo SAP Support ____________________ Information for SAP 16.07.2026 11:14:30 SM_ITSM Bom dia, Antes da nova lei o IRRF era calculado corretamente. Pelo que vi só mudou a forma de calculo. Implementamos varias SAP notes sobre o assunto e fiz as configurações solicitadas na SAP note 3696020. Não é possível calcular o imposto no momento do lançamento da nota, pois ele é cumulativo de acordo com o pagamento. Notas sobre o tema: 3696020 3696600 3699937 3698071 3699220 3694483 3704495 3705756 3696029 3697535 3700214 3701949 3714177 3697572 3695563 3699435

entry.title: Re: System-Defined Breakdown Category ID Not Transported...
entry.content:
Dear @Rama_Krishna77,

No additional manual configuration or manual creation of breakdown category 0SH – Statistical Shares is required

For activity-based Consolidation of Investments, SAP creates the technical statistical FS items and their corresponding breakdown categories automatically when task 2101 – ABCOI Consolidation of Investments is executed for the first time. SAP recommends running this task initially in test mode. The generated statistical FS items have the prefix while their assigned breakdown categories have the prefix. These system-generated objects must not be changed manually

More details below:

SAP Help – Financial Statement Items

SAP KBA 3360547 specifically covers situations where statistical breakdown categories are inconsistent between systems. SAP confirms that the missing categories are created automatically after task 2101 is executed in the Consolidation Monitor in test mode or update mode

Therefore the presence of 0SH in the H0R/100 customizing tenant but its absence in the HS7/100 test tenant does not necessarily indicate a missing customizing transport. It may mean that task 2101 has already been executed in H0R/100 but has not yet been executed in HS7/100

More details below:

SAP KBA 3360547 – Some Breakdown Categories for Statistical FS Items Inconsistent Between the Test and Production System

Summary:

No separate configuration is required. When activity-based Consolidation of Investments is used, task 2101 generates the missing technical statistical breakdown categories locally in the relevant tenant

IMPORTANT: If 0SH is still missing in HS7/100 after task 2101 has been completed successfully in test or update mode raise an SAP support case under component FIN-CS-COR-COI-2CL and provide the task execution log and screenshots from both tenants

More details below:

SAP for Me – Create a Support Case

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: SAP Community Nigeria Event Summary - Key Takeaways
entry.content:
From Classical SAP to AI Architect: Solving Bottlenecks with the Adaptive Semantic Extraction Framework.

Speaker: Mukesh Kumar

Many classical SAP professionals are looking for the best way to start their journey into AI and SAP BTP.  In this keynote, we explored what Generative AI means for the future of SAP consulting and how professionals can pivot their skills to deliver massive business value. After a foundational overview of SAP BTP, guest speaker Mukesh Kumar shared a real-world success story using the Adaptive Semantic Extraction Framework to fully automate complex sales orders and resolve active audit findings

When Classical SAP Met Its AI Moment

Picture a Global Agri Business Company drowning in paperwork. Every day, thousands of purchase orders pour in  some as PDFs, some as emails, some as messy spreadsheets, each one shaped differently. Sales teams were burning 40-60% of their time just typing this information into SAP S/4HANA by hand. Orders that should have taken minutes were taking 24-48 hours. Mistakes crept in wrong part numbers, wrong quantities, wrong pricing and customers noticed. When seasonal demand spiked, there was only one answer: hire more people. It wasn't sustainable, and it wasn't scalable.

This was the real-world problem Mukesh Kumar opened with at our SAP Community Nigeria session on 22 July  and it's the kind of story every practitioner in the room recognized instantly, because most of us have lived some version of it.

From there, Mukesh took the audience on a journey through SAP's AI strategy, boiled down to two simple ideas. Embedded AI is the AI that's already sitting inside the tools we use every day S/4HANA, SuccessFactors, Ariba  no new project, no new team, just switch it on. Think of it like your phone quietly updating overnight; you didn't build the feature, you just start using it. Then there's Custom AI on BTP, for the harder 20% of problems that are unique to your business  the professional kitchen, as he put it, versus the frozen dinner of embedded AI.

But the heart of the session was watching that agri-business company's problem get solved, step by step. Mukesh walked through the Adaptive Semantic Extraction Framework (ASEF) a five-stage pipeline that takes a messy purchase order, understands what it actually means using generative AI (not brittle OCR or RPA that breaks the moment a layout changes), checks it against SAP master data, creates the sales order automatically, and only pulls in a human when something looks genuinely uncertain.

The payoff was dramatic: over 90% less manual effort, more than 1,000 person-hours saved a year, over 70% of orders now running through automation, and a 30% jump in transaction volume, all without adding headcount during peak season. And the story didn't end with agriculture. The same framework was later reused for a Fashion & Apparel company with comparable results, proving this wasn't a one off fix but a repeatable pattern one Mukesh showed could apply just as well to manufacturing, financial services, healthcare, and retail.

Mukesh closed with three lessons that stuck with the room: start with the business pain, not the technology. Build for reuse, not a single use case. And never let AI run fully unsupervised human oversight is what earns the business's trust.

For a room full of hands on consultants many still deeply rooted in classical SAP ways of working  the session offered something valuable: proof that the AI shift doesn't mean abandoning what we know, but building on it. A few are already fully leaning into this AI wave; many more are just beginning that journey. Either way, this session gave everyone a concrete blueprint to start from

If you weren't able to attend or would like to revisit any of the topics - you can find the recordings of both sessions below.

Presentation Slide 

Event Recording 

Resources From Mukesh Kumar

Embedded AI Use cases:
Customers can explore all available embedded AI use cases through the SAP Discovery Center AI Feature Catalog ( https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/ai-catalog/ ), which provides a comprehensive, searchable library of embedded AI capabilities across SAP's product portfolio, including use case descriptions, business benefits, and activation guidance.
 
External Enablement Courses: BTP / BAIP for SAP Practitioners
Here is a curated list of externally available enablement courses and learning resources on SAP BTP and Business AI Platform (BAIP) for SAP practitioners:
Foundational BTP Courses
  • Course Name: Discovering SAP Business Technology Platform
  • Platform: SAP Learning (Free, Self-Paced)
  • Description: Introduction to SAP BTP and the Intelligent Enterprise strategy — ideal starting point for all practitioners.
  • Link: https://learning.sap.com/courses/technical-implementation-and-operation-i-of-sap-s-4hana-and-sap-business-suite/describing-sap-business-technology-platform
  • Course Name: Exploring SAP Business Technology Platform
  • Platform: SAP Learning (Free, ~4hr 44min, Beginner)
  • Description: Covers BTP Strategy, Architecture & Services, Application Development, Integration, AI & Data Foundation, and Security Fundamentals across 6 units. Suitable for Architects, Consultants, and Business Users. Also available in German, Spanish, French, Japanese, Korean, Portuguese, and Chinese.
  • Link: https://learning.sap.com/courses/exploring-sap-business-technology-platform
Business AI / BAIP Courses
  • Course Name: Exploring the SAP Business AI Portfolio
  • Platform: SAP Learning (Certification Learning Path)
  • Description: Recognized learning path covering SAP's Business AI strategy and portfolio — includes certification preparation.
  • Link: https://learning.sap.com/courses/discovering-sap-business-ai

entry.title: Re: Classic Kanban with External Procurement and Cloud ER...
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, the recommended approach for supplying a WM-managed storage location via Kanban is a two-step process combining two scope items:

4B4 – Material Replenishment with Kanban: Stock Transfer https://me.sap.com/processnavigator/SolS/EARL_SolS-013/2608/SolP/4B4?region=DE This scope item covers the Kanban control cycle configuration using a stock transfer replenishment strategy. Your Production Supply Area (PSA) is supplied from a WM-managed storage location through a warehouse-managed transfer process — not a direct purchase order trigger.

3BR – Warehouse Inbound Processing https://me.sap.com/processnavigator/SolS/EARL_SolS-013/2608/SolP/3BR?region=DE Before Kanban replenishment can run, stock must be available in the WM-managed storage location. Use this scope item to complete inbound processing (goods receipt and putaway) so the warehouse holds the stock that Kanban will pull from.

In short: Classic Kanban with a direct external procurement (purchase order) trigger into a WM-managed PSA is not the standard supported path. The supported process separates the two concerns — inbound procurement is handled via 3BR, and Kanban-driven supply into production is handled via 4B4 using a stock transfer strategy.

If you're designing this process, I'd recommend reviewing both test scripts to understand the end-to-end flow.
